title: Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple
tagline: Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple at Kuruvathi (Kannada: ಕುರುವತ್ತಿ) is a temple at the extreme southwestern corner of Hoovina Hadagali taluk, Vijayanagara District, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple at Kuruvathi (Kannada: ಕುರುವತ್ತಿ) is a temple at the extreme southwestern corner of Hoovina Hadagali taluk, Vijayanagara District, Karnataka, India. It is on the bank of Tungabhadra river, 16 km from Halavagalu and 3 km from Mylara.

topPlaces.name: Mylara Lingeshwara Temple
topPlaces.description: Mylara Lingeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to a form of the Hindu god Shiva in Mylara, in the southwest of Hoovina Hadagali taluk, Vijayanagara district, Karnataka, India, 2 km from the Tungabhadra River.

topPlaces.name: Chaudayyadanapura
topPlaces.description: Chaudayyadanapur is a small village in Ranebennur taluk of Haveri District in Karnataka state of India. All facets of Indian civilisation (religion, art, and poetry) are exemplified in an exquisite Mukteshwara temple, located within the village.

topPlaces.name: Guttal
topPlaces.description: Guttal is a town in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Haveri taluk of Haveri district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Havanur
topPlaces.description: Havanur is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Haveri taluk of Haveri district in North Karnataka.

items.note: Mylara Lingeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to a form of the Hindu god Shiva in Mylara, in the southwest of Hoovina Hadagali taluk, Vijayanagara district, Karnataka, India, 2 km from the Tungabhadra River.

Chaudayyadanapur is a small village in Ranebennur taluk of Haveri District in Karnataka state of India. All facets of Indian civilisation (religion, art, and poetry) are exemplified in an exquisite Mukteshwara temple, located within the village.
Guttal is a town in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Haveri taluk of Haveri district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (92 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kuruvathi Basaveshwara temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mylara Lingeshwara Temple, Chaudayyadanapura, Guttal, Havanur.
